Article 1.heading: The Arbitration Law shall apply and be construed with respect to a trust arbitration, as stated hereunder

Article 18.heading: Role of the Court in administration of trust

Article 45.heading: Beneficiaries of a trust (1) A beneficiary shall be:

Article 2.heading: In the Arbitration Law, “dispute” includes an administration question

Article 4.heading: Where in the Arbitration Law reference is made to a matter agreed between the parties to an arbitration agreement (including a matter which may be authorised, chosen, conferred, designated, nominated or vested by the parties) that matter shall (except where no effective provision is made) be determined as provided in the trust instrument

Article 5.heading: Neither Article 12 of the Arbitration Law nor any rule of law or construction treating an arbitration agreement separate to any agreement of which it is a part shall apply in relation to a trust arbitration

Article 6.heading: The term “action” in Article 13 of the Arbitration Law includes an application or other reference to the Court concerning an administration question which the trust instrument requires to be submitted to arbitration and a stay of that application or other reference may be sought by any of the parties in relation to the trust, whether or not a party to that application or other reference

Article 7.heading: In any application or other reference to the Court referred to in paragraph 6, the Court may stay the proceedings on its own volition unless all parties in relation to the trust affected by the application are before it or are represented by persons before it

Article 8.heading: Article 38 of the Arbitration Law shall apply as if it included the following Articles:

Article 9.heading: For the purposes of enforcing an arbitral award under Article 41(2)(a)(iii) of the Arbitration Law, the term “dispute” includes an administration question

Where in the Arbitration Law reference i.heading: Where in the Arbitration Law reference is made to a matter agreed between the parties to an arbitration agreement (including a matter which may be authorised, chosen, conferred, designated, nominated or vested by the parties) that matter shall (except where no effective provision is made) be determined as provided in the trust instrument

Neither Article 12 of the Arbitration La.heading: Neither Article 12 of the Arbitration Law nor any rule of law or construction treating an arbitration agreement separate to any agreement of which it is a part shall apply in relation to a trust arbitration

The term “action” in Article 13 of the A.heading: The term “action” in Article 13 of the Arbitration Law includes an application or other reference to the Court concerning an administration question which the trust instrument requires to be submitted to arbitration and a stay of that application or other reference may be sought by any of the parties in relation to the trust, whether or not a party to that application or other reference

In any application or other reference to.heading: In any application or other reference to the Court referred to in paragraph 6, the Court may stay the proceedings on its own volition unless all parties in relation to the trust affected by the application are before it or are represented by persons before it
